title: Into the Breach Design Postmortem
creator: Matthew Davis
description: Matthew Davis reconstructs the long search for Into the Breach’s design, including discarded prototypes, perfect-information combat, board size, enemy intent, time pressure, difficulty, and extensive simplification.
useful: It demonstrates that a coherent ruleset can emerge through years of cuts and reframing rather than from one complete initial idea.
audience: Systems designers and small teams working through prototypes that are promising but not yet coherent.
learning: Use constraints to clarify a tactical design
Communicate enemy intent through perfect information
Recognize simplification as a major design activity
